items: Markets reopened after the Independence Day holiday with AI shares trying to recover from two sessions of heavy semiconductor selling.
Oil and Treasury yields were easing modestly.
The Dow had ended the prior session at a record while the Nasdaq had been pressured by chip liquidation.
scheduled_events: No dominant scheduled U.S. macro release

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Broadcom and other AI names rebounded, lifting the Nasdaq and S&P 500.
The majority of S&P 500 stocks still fell, showing narrow leadership.
Easing oil and yields reduced macro pressure.
causal_narrative: The session was a narrow AI-led rebound rather than broad risk-on confirmation. Falling oil and yields created room for growth shares to recover, but weak breadth meant the move depended heavily on megacap and semiconductor leadership.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
